top_vulnerabilities: {'summary': 'Januscape flaw in Linux KVM hypervisor enables virtual machine escape and host code execution.', 'severity': 'critical', 'affected_products': ['Linux Kernel', 'KVM Hypervisor', 'Intel Systems', 'AMD Systems'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': '9.8', 'workarounds': ['Restrict guest OS privileges and disable unnecessary services', 'Implement strict network segmentation and firewall rules for VMs', 'Disable KVM hardware acceleration features if not strictly required', 'Monitor host systems for unexpected process execution originating from guest contexts'], 'references': ['https://www.securityweek.com/linux-kernel-vulnerability-allows-vm-escape-on-intel-and-amd-systems'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': "The Januscape vulnerability is a flaw within the Linux Kernel's KVM (Kernel-based Virtual Machine) hypervisor. It allows an attacker with access to a guest virtual machine to break out of the virtualized environment and execute arbitrary code on the underlying host system. The flaw impacts systems utilizing both Intel and AMD processor architectures, compromising the fundamental isolation boundary of the hypervisor.", 'attack_vector': 'local', 'privileges_required': 'low', 'exploit_maturity': 'proof-of-concept', 'availability_impact': 'high', 'remediation': 'Apply the latest security patches and kernel updates provided by Linux distribution vendors and the KVM maintainers to address the hypervisor flaw.', 'exploit_available': 'unknown', 'cve_id': 'CVE-XXXX-XXXX', 'cvss_vector': 'CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H', 'user_interaction': 'none', 'scope': 'changed', 'confidentiality_impact': 'high', 'published_date': '2026-07-07', 'discovery_date': ''}

{'summary': 'Unpatched authentication backdoor in Tenda router firmware allows attackers to bypass login and gain full administrative access using a hidden password.', 'severity': 'critical', 'affected_products': ['Tenda FH1201', 'Tenda W15E', 'Tenda AC10', 'Tenda AC5', 'Tenda AC6'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': '', 'workarounds': ['Disable remote management on the device to prevent internet-based exploitation', 'Change the default LAN IP address to reduce exposure to automated scanners'], 'references': ['CERT/CC Advisory for CVE-2026-11405', 'Securityaffairs: Hidden Tenda Router Backdoor Grants Admin Access, No Patch Available'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': 'The login() function in the web server binary /bin/httpd contains an undocumented authentication backdoor. After a standard MD5 password hash check fails, the function retrieves an alternate password from the device configuration via GetValue("sys.rzadmin.password"). It performs a direct plaintext strcmp() comparison between the user-supplied password and this stored value. A successful match grants role=2 (administrative access) and creates a valid session, completely bypassing the configured credentials. The username field is ignored. This backdoor is compiled directly into the firmware binary and cannot be disabled via the management interface.', 'attack_vector': 'network', 'privileges_required': 'none', 'exploit_maturity': 'functional', 'availability_impact': 'high', 'remediation': 'No official patch is currently available. Tenda has not acknowledged the vulnerability or provided a fix timeline. Users must monitor for official firmware updates that remove the backdoor code path entirely.', 'exploit_available': 'yes', 'cve_id': 'CVE-2026-11405', 'cvss_vector': '', 'user_interaction': 'none', 'scope': 'changed', 'confidentiality_impact': 'high', 'published_date': '2026-07-07', 'discovery_date': ''}

{'summary': 'Abuse of legitimate Windows administrative tools and compromised privileged identities for lateral movement, combined with exploitation of vulnerable drivers to disable EDR and backup systems.', 'severity': 'critical', 'affected_products': ['Windows OS', 'Active Directory', 'VMware ESXi', 'Linux environments', 'Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) solutions', 'Backup platforms'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': '', 'workarounds': ['Restrict PsExec, WMIC, and PowerShell remoting via Group Policy/AppLocker', 'Enable and enforce immutable backups on isolated networks', 'Implement privileged access management (PAM) solutions', 'Deploy network segmentation and zero-trust architecture'], 'references': ['Picus Security Report on The Gentlemen', 'Microsoft Threat Intelligence Analysis', 'IDC Cybersecurity Services Research', 'ESET Research on EDR-killer toolsets'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': 'The Gentlemen ransomware campaign leverages compromised administrative credentials and excessive privileges to move laterally across enterprise networks. It abuses trusted Windows management utilities (PsExec, WMIC, scheduled tasks, Windows services, PowerShell remoting, WMI) to propagate. Prior to encryption, it targets security and recovery infrastructure by disabling Microsoft Defender, deleting volume shadow copies, removing forensic artifacts, and stopping database, backup, and virtualization services. The campaign also utilizes a mature EDR-killer toolset that abuses vulnerable kernel drivers to disrupt endpoint security. Encryption uses a hybrid Curve25519 and XChaCha20 scheme, followed by double extortion tactics.', 'attack_vector': 'network', 'privileges_required': 'high', 'exploit_maturity': 'functional', 'availability_impact': 'high', 'remediation': 'Implement phishing-resistant MFA for all privileged accounts. Enforce strict least-privilege access and identity governance. Deploy network segmentation to limit lateral movement paths. Isolate backup systems on separate networks with immutable storage and independent authentication. Regularly test recovery procedures and adversary emulation. Patch and monitor vulnerable kernel drivers. Restrict execution of administrative tools like PsExec and WMI to authorized management stations only.', 'exploit_available': 'yes', 'cve_id': '', 'cvss_vector': '', 'user_interaction': 'none', 'scope': 'changed', 'confidentiality_impact': 'high', 'published_date': '2026-07-07', 'discovery_date': ''}

{'summary': 'Critical security flaw in Roundcube webmail software allowing attackers to siphon credentials.', 'severity': 'critical', 'affected_products': ['Roundcube webmail software'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': 9.3, 'workarounds': [], 'references': ['Thehackernews: Suspected China-Aligned Hackers Exploit Roundcube Flaws Against Universities'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': 'A critical vulnerability in the open-source Roundcube webmail application that allows remote attackers to exploit the software to steal user credentials. The flaw has been actively exploited in the wild by a suspected China-aligned threat group targeting academic institutions.', 'attack_vector': 'network', 'privileges_required': 'none', 'exploit_maturity': 'high', 'availability_impact': 'low', 'remediation': 'Update Roundcube webmail software to the latest patched version immediately.', 'exploit_available': 'yes', 'cve_id': 'CVE-2024-42009', 'cvss_vector': '', 'user_interaction': 'none', 'scope': 'changed', 'confidentiality_impact': 'high', 'published_date': '', 'discovery_date': ''}

{'summary': 'Social engineering campaign leveraging fake IT support calls on Microsoft Teams to trick users into installing remote administration tools and the EtherRAT remote access trojan.', 'severity': 'high', 'affected_products': ['Microsoft Teams', 'Windows', 'Linux', 'macOS', 'HopToDesk', 'AnyDesk'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': 0.0, 'workarounds': ['Disable cross-tenant Teams chats if not required for business operations', 'Enforce GPO/MDM restrictions on installing remote desktop software', "Monitor Teams audit logs for 'External unfamiliar' contact tags"], 'references': ['Theregister: Fake IT bods on Microsoft Teams coax workers into installing malware', 'Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 Research'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': "Attackers initiate contact via phishing emails disguised as employee surveys, followed by Microsoft Teams calls from accounts tagged as 'External unfamiliar'. During the call, attackers impersonate IT support and persuade victims to grant remote control and install legitimate remote administration tools like HopToDesk or AnyDesk. An MSI package is subsequently downloaded to install EtherRAT, a cross-platform Node.js RAT. EtherRAT retrieves its C2 server from an Ethereum smart contract, with a fallback domain. The malware enables command execution, data theft, file manipulation, and persistent access. Forensic analysis reveals Teams generates 'CtrlVirtualCursorWin_*' files during remote sessions. The campaign also references prior use of the React2Shell vulnerability.", 'attack_vector': 'network', 'privileges_required': 'none', 'exploit_maturity': 'high', 'availability_impact': 'high', 'remediation': 'Implement strict application control policies to block unauthorized remote administration tools and MSI installations. Enforce multi-factor authentication and restrict cross-tenant Microsoft Teams communications. Conduct regular security awareness training focusing on social engineering and fake IT support scenarios. Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to monitor for suspicious remote control activity and EtherRAT indicators.', 'exploit_available': 'yes', 'cve_id': '', 'cvss_vector': '', 'user_interaction': 'required', 'scope': 'changed', 'confidentiality_impact': 'high', 'published_date': '2026-07-07', 'discovery_date': ''}
